title: Trade-offs between enzyme fitness and solubility illuminated by deep mutational scanning

primary_findings.key_insight: 5-10% of single missense mutations improve solubility, matching theoretical predictions
primary_findings.trade_off_characterization: Solubility-enhancing mutations often disrupt catalytic activity
primary_findings.correlation_success: YSD screen explains 37% of fitness landscape variance

solubility_analysis.beneficial_mutations: 5-10% of single missense mutations enhance solubility
solubility_analysis.stability_effect: Most beneficial mutations have modest stabilizing effect
solubility_analysis.implication: Multiple mutations typically needed for significant solubility improvement

fitness_retention_prediction.accuracy: 90% for predicting solubility-enhancement while maintaining wild-type fitness
predictive_factors: Evolutionary conservation (positive correlation with fitness retention)
Distance from active site (positive correlation with fitness retention)
Contact number (negative correlation with fitness retention)

trade_off_characterization.observation: Solubility-enhancing mutations enriched at/near active site residues
trade_off_characterization.consequence: Most such mutations deleterious for activity
trade_off_characterization.alternative_strategy: Back-to-consensus mutations maintain function while improving stability

structural_insights.crystallography: Determined structure of solubility-enhanced LGK mutant
structural_insights.biophysical_basis: Revealed molecular mechanisms of stability-activity trade-offs

biotechnological_applications.biocatalysts: Improved solubility increases total turnover number
biotechnological_applications.therapeutics: Improved stability for formulation and storage
